name: Quincy Postal Employees
description_short: Quincy Postal Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving postal workers and their families in Quincy, Illinois since 1930, offering savings accounts, share certificates, and consumer loans.
description_long: Quincy Postal Employees Credit Union was established in 1930 as a 501(c)(14) nonprofit credit union dedicated to serving postal employees and their families in Quincy, Illinois. As a member-owned cooperative, the organization operates under the mission of helping members manage their financial well-being through accessible financial services and encouraging thrift among its membership. The credit union provides a focused range of financial products including savings accounts, share certificates (CDs), and consumer loans to qualified members. As of their most recent 2024 filings, they serve 373 members with savings accounts, 21 members with share certificates, and have issued consumer loans to 51 members. The organization operates with a lean structure of 2 employees and manages total assets of $2.53 million, generating $128,810 in total revenues for 2024 with investment income and dividends comprising the majority of their revenue stream. What distinguishes Quincy Postal Employees Credit Union is its hyper-local focus and occupational membership requirement. Unlike large national credit unions, this organization is intentionally small and serves a specific employee group—postal workers—which allows for personalized service and alignment with the financial needs of that community. The credit union has maintained continuous operation for 95 years, demonstrating stability and commitment to its niche membership base. However, prospective members should understand this is a very small, specialized credit union with limited capacity and services. With only 2 employees, 373 total savings members, and $2.53 million in assets, this organization lacks the technological infrastructure, breadth of services, and financial resources of larger credit unions. Membership eligibility is restricted to postal employees and their families, and the organization's small size means limited hours, fewer branch options, and potentially slower adoption of modern banking conveniences.
pros: Established 1930—95+ years of continuous operation demonstrates long-term stability
Membership restricted to postal employees creates aligned community focused on specific occupational needs
Low consumer loan rates—credit union model ensures reasonable rates vs. commercial lenders
Share certificates available—allowing members to earn investment income on deposits
Member-owned cooperative structure—no external shareholders, profits benefit members
Personalized service model with small staff dedicated to member relationships
401(c)(14) nonprofit status ensures mission-driven operations focused on member benefit
cons: Extremely limited membership eligibility—only postal employees and their immediate families qualify
Very small organization with only 2 employees—limited service hours and availability
Minimal assets ($2.53M) and member base (373 savings members)—cannot compete with large credit unions on technology or product range
No online banking mentioned or modern fintech integrations—likely outdated digital infrastructure
Geographic restriction to Quincy, Illinois—no remote access or multi-location convenience
best_for: Postal employees and their families in the Quincy, Illinois area seeking a community-focused credit union
Members prioritizing personal relationships and local financial institutions over digital convenience
Conservative savers looking for share certificates and basic savings products with reasonable rates
diagnosis: Quincy Postal Employees Credit Union is best for postal employees and their families in Quincy, Illinois who value community banking and personalized service over modern digital banking. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to postal workers, the organization is extremely small with minimal technological infrastructure, and it offers only basic traditional credit union products—not suitable for consumers seeking comprehensive banking services or online-first banking experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
